title: compile() does two separate script tree-walks
description: compile() performs two independent O(n) tree-walks over the parsed script: check_extension_use() (lib.rs ~155-157) and validate_stmt() via validate_script() (lib.rs ~161). Both recurse into Block and TestList nodes. They could be merged into a single combined pass, halving allocations and cache misses during compilation. Impact is proportional to script size; for typical mail filter scripts (tens of statements) this is negligible, but for large generated scripts or compile-time benchmarks it is a constant-factor regression.
notes: Two O(n) tree-walks is constant factor only; scripts are small. A future refactor to merge validate_stmt and check_extension_use is valid but not urgent.
